name: Mavel School Bus Corp
districts_or_customers: Likely Miami-Dade County Public Schools area (Hialeah-based); not found on the M-DCPS approved private bus company list reviewed, but consistent with the cluster of small Hialeah private school-bus contractors (J&M Vera, Maranata, Franmar, Damas, A. Oliveros, Ruiz, etc.)
recent_news: No M&A, ownership, or news found. No website or public web footprint located.
fit_notes: Sub-scale single-location Hialeah operator (~31 employees, ~$1.4M revenue, ~est. 10-20 buses). Owner-named entity suggests family-owned, possible tuck-in if Summit consolidates the highly fragmented Miami-Dade private school-bus contractor pool, but very small standalone.

name: CTI School Bus Contract Svc
districts_or_customers: Likely greater Pittsburgh / South Hills area school districts (Bethel Park, Mt. Lebanon, Upper St. Clair area); no specific district contract confirmed in web sources
recent_news: No M&A, ownership, news, or public web footprint located. No website found; not on Pittsburgh Public Schools' published carrier list reviewed.
fit_notes: Very small Pittsburgh-area school bus contractor (~31 employees, ~$1.4M revenue). Limited public footprint; would need direct outreach to confirm operator status, fleet size, and ownership before pursuing as tuck-in. Allegheny County is a fragmented school-bus market with many small contractors.

name: Schenectady Bus Con LTD
districts_or_customers: None - not an operating school bus contractor
recent_news: Misclassification: 'Schenectady Bus-Con, Ltd.' operates as Consumer Pak, an addressing/mailing/business services company (www.consumerpak.com), not a student transportation operator. Phone (518) 399-9545 matches the source record. Buzzfile lists ~3 employees and ~$248K annual revenue in 'Addressing Service' industry. Should be removed from the school-bus target list.
fit_notes: NOT a target. Despite the 'Bus Con' name suggesting bus contracting, this is an addressing/direct-mail services business (Consumer Pak). Recommend dropping from the Summit acquisition universe.

name: School Days Inc
districts_or_customers: NYC Department of Education / Office of Pupil Transportation (OPT). Sister/co-located entity Tufaro Transit Co. Inc. operates from same 225 Van Brunt St, Brooklyn address with same (718) 237-9200 phone; FMCSA shows 41 power units and 41 drivers under USDOT 350708 / MC 165111. NLRB cases (2013) indicate a unionized Brooklyn pupil-transport workforce typical of NYC OPT contractors.
recent_news: No recent M&A. Long-tenured family operation - Joseph Tufaro has owned the business since 1977 (47+ years), a strong succession signal. Litigation history includes Tufaro Transit Co. v. Board of Ed. of NYC (early 1980s) and 2013 NLRB cases, consistent with a long-standing NYC OPT contractor.
fit_notes: Attractive Brooklyn family tuck-in: ~41-bus NYC DOE OPT contractor, family-owned for nearly 50 years under aging owner (Joseph Tufaro), strong succession signal. School Days Inc and Tufaro Transit appear to be co-located affiliated entities (same address/phone) - any deal likely needs to encompass both. NYC OPT contracts (subject to EPP litigation and re-bidding) are a sensitive but consolidated market where platforms like Reliant/Beacon, NellBus, and Pioneer have been active acquirers.

name: Damas Service School Bus
districts_or_customers: Miami-Dade area (Hialeah-based). Listed on Yelp at 1510 W 35th Pl, Hialeah, FL 33012. Not found on the M-DCPS approved private bus company list reviewed.
recent_news: No M&A, ownership, news, or public web footprint located beyond a Yelp/Yellow Pages listing. No website found.
fit_notes: Very small Hialeah operator (~18 employees, ~$1.4M revenue, likely 5-15 buses). Hispanic-owned (presumed from name) family operator in the highly fragmented Miami-Dade private school-bus / field trip contractor pool. Sub-scale standalone; would only be relevant in a roll-up strategy alongside peers (J&M Vera, Maranata, Franmar, A. Oliveros, J. Ruiz, etc.).
